title: Can neighbourhood apps build real communities?
explanation:
Can neighbourhood apps build real communities?

In many towns and cities, local residents now use neighbourhood apps to share information, ask for help and organise events. A message about a lost pet can spread within minutes, and someone who needs a ladder or a babysitter may find support nearby. Supporters say these apps help strangers trust each other and make communities more practical. Critics argue that online communication can never fully replace real conversations and may even increase suspicion when people share too many complaints. Whether these apps strengthen a neighbourhood seems to depend on how respectfully people use them.

questions.text: Neighbourhood apps allow residents to [*share information quickly|book flights|watch documentaries].
questions.feedback: That is one of their main uses.

questions.text: Someone who needs a ladder may find [*local support|legal advice|a new job].
Critics think online communication cannot fully replace [*real conversations|public transport|formal education].
The effect of the apps depends on how [*respectfully|expensively|secretly] people use them.

exercise_sets.title: Exercise 2
exercise_sets.instructions: Choose the correct option for each gap.

questions.text: A message about a lost ____ can spread very quickly. | *pet | receipt | deadline
Supporters say the apps make communities more ____.  | *practical | private | formal
Too many complaints may increase ____.  | *suspicion | income | fitness
The apps can also help people organise local ____.  | *events | laws | storms
The writer does not claim the apps are [*completely good or bad|useless|dangerous in all cases].
The passage is mainly about [*technology and community|airport delays|medical treatment].
The text suggests behaviour matters as much as the [*technology|weather|budget].
One positive effect mentioned is increased [*trust|competition|advertising].
questions.feedback: Supporters say strangers trust each other more.
